narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A payment service whose external-provider integration uses basic cross-region VPC peering. Network latency to the provider spikes during business hours, cascading into elevated compute-tier application latency. Compute itself is correctly sized; the bottleneck is at the network boundary.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests the Network Analyst's ability to identify a network-layer issue and the Cross-Tier Evaluator's ability to recognize the cascade into compute. The fix is network topology (PrivateLink) plus retry policy.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Switch from basic VPC peering to AWS PrivateLink for the payment-provider integration and add retries with exponential backoff.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation identifies the network as the root cause. A great recommendation specifies the PrivateLink swap, pairs it with retry policy improvement, and notes that compute scaling would not address the root cause.

before_after_evidence.config_before: basic_vpc_peering for payment provider, no retry policy
before_after_evidence.config_after: AWS PrivateLink for payment provider + retries with exponential backoff
before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: network_p95_latency_ms fell below 80ms business-hours, application_p95_latency_ms fell below 220ms, network_error_rate dropped below 0.002, SLA 99.9% preserved.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across payment-service cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Switch from basic VPC peering to AWS PrivateLink for the payment-provider integration. Add retries with exponential backoff at the service boundary.
target_recommendation.expected_performance_impact: network_p95_latency_ms drops to under 80ms; application_p95_latency_ms drops to under 220ms
target_recommendation.expected_reliability_impact: SLA 99.9% preserved; error rate drops below 0.2%
